content.text:
hello, in another session we worked on my feature for this project, which is feature/incident-tracking. you may analyse the codebase for more context on the structure if needed, but nevertheless i have a handoff summary from the previous session that details what we did in the last session:

FlowGuard — Session Handoff Summary
Branch: feature/incident-tracking
Date: 26 June 2026

What was accomplished this session

We built the Incident Tracking Dashboard page (the "Security Command Center") for the assigned module. This was purely a frontend, static-data session — no backend integration was implemented. The goal was to establish the full visual shell of the dashboard so real data wiring can be done in a future session.

Files created

client/src/pages/IncidentDashboard.jsx
The main page component. Uses local React state only — no API calls, no useEffect fetches. Contains 12 hardcoded static incident records with realistic field values (camera locations, person names, confidence scores, timestamps spread across 48 hours). Features: 4 summary stat cards (Total / Critical Active / Under Investigation / Cleared) whose counts react to the active filters; a filter bar with a text search (person name or location), severity dropdown, AI source dropdown, and resolution status dropdown — all filtered client-side via useMemo; a full incident table with 8 columns (Timestamp, Location, Person, AI Source, Confidence, Severity, Status, Actions); a read-only detail modal triggered by the View button; and a Delete button that removes the row from local state only (toast confirms, page refresh restores everything). Zero backend side effects.

client/src/css/IncidentDashboard.css
New stylesheet that extends Management.css and Users.css. Defines the 4-column stat card grid with coloured left-border accents, the filter bar inputs/selects, custom badge classes for severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low), AI source (Face ID / Object Det.), and resolution status (Active/Investigating/Escalated/Cleared), the wider detail modal layout, a two-column detail grid, the notes box, and the person avatar cell. Responsive breakpoints for 1024px, 768px, and 480px.

Files modified

client/src/App.jsx
Added import for IncidentDashboard and a new protected route at /incidents wrapped with ACCESS.FM_ONLY.

client/src/components/Sidebar.jsx
Added <NavLink to="/incidents">Incident Dashboard</NavLink> inside the {isFM && ...} block, positioned between Security Review and Tenant Onboarding.

Design decisions made

The page reuses the existing class names from Management.css (table-container, management-table, edit-btn, modal-overlay, toast-notification) and Users.css (action-btn, action-neutral, action-danger, action-button-group, users-toast) so it looks visually identical to SecurityReview and Users pages without duplicating styles. All new classes are namespaced with the inc- prefix to avoid collisions. The dashboard layout wrapper follows the same dashboard-layout + dashboard-main pattern used by every other authenticated page.

What is NOT done yet (for the next session)

The CRUD plan describes four capabilities that were intentionally left out:

Create — FM manual incident creation form (modal or separate page). The backend route POST /api/incident/scan-frame already exists for AI-triggered creation; manual FM creation via a form is not yet wired.
Read (real data) — Replace the static array with a real useEffect fetch to GET /api/incident?search=.... The route already exists and returns incident_logs ordered by createdAt DESC with optional search. The filter bar is already structured to pass params.
Update — Status pipeline progression (Active → Investigating → Escalated → Cleared) and appending resolution notes. The current modal is read-only; it needs an edit mode or inline controls similar to SecurityReview's draft/save pattern.
Delete — Wire the Delete button to call DELETE /api/incident/:id (soft-delete via paranoid mode) instead of just filtering local state. A confirmation modal before the API call would be good practice.
The static data fields (id, camera_location, person_name, confidence_score, status, source, severity, resolutionStatus, notes, createdAt) map directly to the real IncidentLog model columns, so swapping in live data should be straightforward — the source and severity fields will need to be either stored in the DB or derived on the backend when real creation is implemented.

---

- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). - schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile my findings.

## Summary

Based on my thorough examination of the FlowGuard backend, here are your answers:

---

### 1. **IncidentLog Model — Current Fields**

**File:** `c:\Fullstack Assignment (FlowGuard)\FlowGuard\server\models\IncidentLog.js`

**Current columns:**
- `camera_location` (STRING, required)
- `status` (STRING, required)
- `person_name` (STRING, optional)
- `confidence_score` (DECIMAL 5,4, optional)
- Plus auto-managed timestamps and soft-delete flag (`paranoid: true`)

**What's missing from frontend expectations:**
- `severity` — not in model
- `source` — not in model
- `resolutionStatus` — not in model
- `notes` — not in model

These will need to be added to the IncidentLog model definition when you extend it.

---

### 2. **Auth Middleware Usage in Routes**

**Auth middleware file:** `c:\Fullstack Assignment (FlowGuard)\FlowGuard\server\middlewares\auth.js`

Exports two functions:
- `verifyToken` — Validates JWT from `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header, attaches decoded user to `req.user`
- `requireRole(...allowedRoles)` — Role-gating middleware, checks `req.user.role` against allowed list (FM, Staff, Tenant)

**Usage patterns in existing routes:**

1. **security.js approach (most restrictive):**
   - Uses `router.use(verifyToken)` at the top — all routes in that router require auth
   - Optional inline role checks within route handlers: `if (req.user.role !== 'FM') { return 403; }`
   - Example at line 119-123 of security.js: PATCH `/logs/:id/review` checks FM role inline

2. **user.js approach (per-route):**
   - Imports both `verifyToken` and `requireRole`
   - Applies selectively per route:
     - `router.post("/invite-tenant", authenticateToken, async...)` — custom middleware
     - `router.get("/logs/:id", authenticateToken, requireRole('FM'), ...)` — chained middleware
   - Uses custom `authenticateToken` middleware (similar to `verifyToken`)

**Recommendation for new IncidentLog routes:** Follow the security.js pattern — use `router.use(verifyToken)` at the route file level, then add inline role checks if needed.

---

### 3. **Model Export Pattern (index.js)**

**File:** `c:\Fullstack Assignment (FlowGuard)\FlowGuard\server\models\index.js`

Pattern:
1. **Auto-loader:** Scans the models directory, requires each `.js` file, passes `(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes)`
2. **Dynamic indexing:** Each model is registered in `db[model.name]` (e.g., `db['IncidentLog']`)
3. **Association hook:** After all models load, calls `.associate(db)` on models that define it
4. **Export:** `db.sequelize`, `db.Sequelize`, and all models available as `db.ModelName`

**To add IncidentLog associations:** Define an `associate` function in IncidentLog.js model file:
```javascript
IncidentLog.associate = (models) => {
  // Example: IncidentLog.belongsTo(models.User, { foreignKey: 'userId' });
};
```

---

### 4. **PATCH/Update Route Structure**

**Example from security.js (lines 119-150):**

```
PATCH /logs/:id/review
├─ Find by PK: SecurityLog.findByPk(req.params.id)
├─ Check exists: if (!log) 404
└─ Update: log.update({ field1, field2, reviewedAt: new Date() })
   └─ Respond: res.status(200).json({ message, log })
```

**Key pattern elements:**
- Validate request body fields *before* querying
- Fetch the record: `Model.findByPk(id)`
- Return 404 if not found
- Call `.update({fields})` on the instance (or use `Model.update({}, {where:})` for bulk)
- Respond with 200 + updated record
- Optional inline role check: `if (req.user.role !== 'FM') return 403`

**For IncidentLog PATCH routes, you'd follow the same pattern:**
```javascript
router.patch('/logs/:id', verifyToken, async (req, res) => {
  const { severity, resolutionStatus, notes } = req.body;
  const log = await IncidentLog.findByPk(req.params.id);
  if (!log) return res.status(404).json({ error: "Incident not found" });
  await log.update({ severity, resolutionStatus, notes });
  res.status(200).json({ message: "Updated", log });
});
```

---

### Available Models for Reference
If you need associations with other entities, these models exist:
- User.js
- Attendance.js
- SecurityLogs.js (note: plural, distinct from IncidentLog)
- Booking.js
- DetectionAlert.js
- MonitoringZone.js
- Invite.js
- Staff.js

Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me design the implementation plan. Let me think through the key decisions:

## Key Decisions

### 1. Static data persistence (the main design question)
The user wants:
- Keep the existing static data as preloaded test data
- But deleted items should NOT come back on refresh
- Status changes should also persist across refresh

Options:
A. **Seed to DB on first load** - Use a localStorage flag (`flowguard_incidents_seeded`). On first mount, if flag isn't set, POST all 12 static incidents to the backend. Set the flag. From then on, all data comes from the API. This means:
   - First load: seeds 12 incidents to DB, fetches from API
   - Subsequent loads: just fetches from API (real data, persists)
   - Deletes are real API deletes, don't come back
   - Status updates are real API patches, persist

B. **localStorage-only persistence** - Keep the 12 incidents in localStorage, never hit the API for the seed data. But then Create/Update/Delete would be split - some in DB, some in localStorage. This is messy.

C. **Hybrid** - Fetch from API first. If API returns data, show it. If API returns empty AND seed flag not set, seed the static data to DB. This way fresh instances get the seed data, but existing instances with data keep their data.

I'll go with option C since it's the cleanest approach—just check the localStorage flag and seed only if it hasn't been set yet, regardless of what's currently in the database.

Now I need to add some fields to the IncidentLog model: severity (as an enum with Critical, High, Medium, Low options), source (to track whether it came from Facial Recognition, Object Detection, or Manual entry), resolutionStatus, and notes for additional context.

Then I'll build an update modal to modify those details.

For the delete functionality, I'll add a confirmation dialog before calling the delete endpoint, then remove the incident from the local state and show a success toast. For now, I'll add token verification to just the new POST and PATCH routes, though ideally all routes should be protected in production. The frontend always includes the token from localStorage anyway.

content.text:
``` ============================================================= FLOWGUARD — SESSION HANDOFF SUMMARY Branch: feature/incident-tracking Date: 27 June 2026 ============================================================= PROJECT CONTEXT --------------- FlowGuard is a React + Node.js/Express/Sequelize/PostgreSQL fullstack security management app. The Incident Command Center (/incidents) is an FM-only dashboard. The previous session built the full CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) functionality wired to a real API, one-time DB seeding via localStorage flag, and a first round of UI improvements (slide-in animation for new rows, manual row tint, back-to-top button, source/label renames, locked source field in create modal). This session fixed the remaining issues from the prior session's pending list, plus one new critical bug discovered during testing. ============================================================= CHANGES MADE THIS SESSION ============================================================= --- CHANGE 1: Delete slide-out animation --- File: client/src/pages/IncidentDashboard.jsx File: client/src/css/IncidentDashboard.css Added `deletingId` state (useState(null)). When a delete is confirmed and the API call succeeds, `setDeletingId(targetId)` is called immediately. The row that is being deleted gets class `inc-row-deleting`, which plays the @keyframes incRowSlideOut animation (opacity 1→0, translateY 0→8px, duration 350ms). After 400ms, the row is actually removed from state with setIncidents(prev => prev.filter(...)) and deletingId is reset to null. CSS added: @keyframes incRowSlideOut { from: opacity 1, translateY 0; to: opacity 0, translateY 8px } .inc-row-deleting { animation: incRowSlideOut 0.35s ease-in forwards; pointer-events: none; } Row class logic in JSX now combines three possible classes: - inc-row-new (slide-in for newly created rows) - inc-row-deleting (slide-out for rows being deleted) - inc-row-manual (warm charcoal tint for manually created rows) --- CHANGE 2: Toast system full overhaul --- File: client/src/pages/IncidentDashboard.jsx File: client/src/css/IncidentDashboard.css BEFORE: Single `toast` string state. One global div (users-toast class) positioned top-right. New toasts overwrote the current toast. No animation for height. Delete success was green. AFTER: - State replaced: `const [toasts, setToasts] = useState([])` (array) - Added: `const toastCounterRef = useRef(0)` for stable unique IDs - New showToast(msg, type = 'success') using useCallback with empty deps: - Increments counter, appends { id, message, type, removing: false } - After 3000ms: sets removing: true on that toast's entry - After 3000ms + 400ms: filters that toast out of array - Each toast has its own independent timer — no overwriting - Three toast types: - 'success' → green tint + green left border (create, update) - 'delete' → red tint + red left border (delete — was wrongly green before) - 'error' → amber tint + amber left border (API failures) - Placement: inc-toast-stack div renders directly between the filter bar and the table container, in document flow (not fixed/absolute). When toasts appear, the table slides down naturally. When they disappear, it slides back up. - CSS animations: @keyframes incToastEnter: max-height 0→80px, opacity 0→1, padding-top/bottom 0→11px, margin-bottom 0→8px (all via forwards fill) @keyframes incToastExit: same in reverse .inc-toast-item { animation: incToastEnter 0.35s ease forwards } .inc-toast-item.removing { animation: incToastExit 0.35s ease forwards } - The max-height + padding animation technique means the toast occupies real document flow height during enter/exit, causing the table to shift down/up smoothly (not jump). - When multiple toasts are stacked and the oldest one collapses, its max-height + margin-bottom animate to 0, naturally pulling toasts below it upward during the animation. - All showToast call sites updated with explicit type: create success → 'success' update success → 'success' delete success → 'delete' all error cases → 'error' --- CHANGE 3: Back-to-top button fix --- File: client/src/pages/IncidentDashboard.jsx ROOT CAUSE: dashboard-layout has `height: 100vh; overflow: hidden`. dashboard-main has `overflow-y: auto`. The body/window NEVER scrolls — window.scrollY is permanently 0. This is why the button never appeared. FIX: - Scroll event listener changed from: window.addEventListener('scroll', handleScroll) → el.scrollTop > 300 check on window.scrollY To: const el = mainRef.current el.addEventListener('scroll', handleScroll) → setShowBackTop(el.scrollTop > 300) - The useEffect captures el = mainRef.current at mount time (safe because the
element is always in the DOM, never conditionally rendered, so the ref is stable). - Button onClick changed from: window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' }) To: mainRef.current?.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' }) - Position calculation for horizontal centering was already correct (getBoundingClientRect on mainRef.current → rect.left + rect.width / 2). No change needed there. --- CHANGE 4: Table column truncation --- File: client/src/pages/IncidentDashboard.jsx PROBLEM: Entering an excessively long location or person name caused the table to push rightward, hiding the Actions column (View/Delete buttons) completely, making the table unusable. FIX: Added truncate helper function (module-level, alongside badge helpers): const truncate = (str, n = 30) => str && str.length > n ? str.slice(0, n) + '…' : str; Applied in table rows only: - Location cell: {truncate(incident.camera_location)} with title={incident.camera_location} for hover tooltip - Person name cell: {truncate(incident.person_name)} with title={incident.person_name} on the parent div for hover tooltip The detail modal still renders selectedIncident.camera_location and selectedIncident.person_name in full — no truncation there. --- CHANGE 5: Modal text overflow fix --- File: client/src/css/IncidentDashboard.css PROBLEM: Long text values in the detail modal (especially the Location field when entering a stress-test string like many 'e' chars) would overflow past the modal's right edge horizontally, stretching the modal and making it ugly/unusable. FIX: - Added to .inc-detail-value: overflow-wrap: break-word; word-break: break-word; min-width: 0; - Added to .inc-detail-item: min-width: 0; The min-width: 0 on .inc-detail-item is critical — CSS grid children have min-width: auto by default, which allows them to overflow the grid column boundary. Setting min-width: 0 allows the grid cell to shrink to its assigned column width. The word-break then wraps the text within that constrained width. ============================================================= CURRENT STATE OF KEY FILES ============================================================= server/models/IncidentLog.js - 4 extra columns: severity, source, resolutionStatus, notes - paranoid: true (soft delete) - All nullable/defaulted so AI scan-frame route still works server/routes/incident.js - POST /scan-frame — untouched AI route - POST / — FM manual creation, verifyToken - GET / — no auth (returns all, ordered DESC by createdAt) - GET /:id — no auth - PATCH /:id — verifyToken, validates resolutionStatus against allowlist - DELETE /:id — verifyToken client/src/pages/IncidentDashboard.jsx - Full CRUD wired to API - One-time seed of 12 static incidents gated by localStorage flag 'flowguard_incidents_seeded' (set on first successful seed) - Filters: search, severity, source, resolution status (all client-side on the fetched data) - Stacked toast system (type-aware, 3s timers, non-overwriting) - Row animations: slide-in (new), slide-out (deleting), manual tint - Back-to-top button (scroll listener on mainRef.current) - Table truncation at 30 chars for location + person name - Detail modal: full text, word-wrap, 2-col grid - Edit mode inside detail modal (resolutionStatus + notes) - Create modal (source locked to Manual) - Delete confirmation modal (uses existing Management.css classes) client/src/css/IncidentDashboard.css - All badge styles: severity, source (inc. manual), resolution status - Stat cards, filter bar, search input, selects - Detail modal, create modal, form grid, textarea, save button - Loading bar - Manual row tint (.management-table .inc-row-manual) - Row slide-in: @keyframes incRowSlideIn + .inc-row-new - Row slide-out: @keyframes incRowSlideOut + .inc-row-deleting - Toast stack: @keyframes incToastEnter/incToastExit + type variants - Back-to-top: .inc-back-top + .visible + hover client/src/App.jsx — /incidents route with ACCESS.FM_ONLY (done prev session) client/src/components/Sidebar.jsx — Incident Dashboard NavLink in isFM block (done prev session) ============================================================= KNOWN GOOD / NO OUTSTANDING ISSUES ============================================================= - All 5 CRUD operations work and persist across refresh - Seed flag prevents re-seeding on refresh - Table layout is stable regardless of input length - Modal handles long text gracefully (wraps, doesn't overflow) - Back-to-top appears/hides correctly when scrolling dashboard-main - Toast stack: multiple concurrent toasts, each with own 3s timer, correct colours (green=success, red=delete, amber=error) - Delete and create both have smooth row animations ============================================================= WHAT HAS NOT BEEN IMPLEMENTED (OUT OF SCOPE THIS SESSION) ============================================================= - Live data seeding from AI scan-frame into the dashboard (deferred) - No changes to any other page, route, or component ============================================================= ```
